name: Pattern Interrupt Openers
summary: Openers designed to break the buyer's expectation of generic sales language so you earn a few extra seconds of real attention.
whenToUseIt: Use pattern interrupt openers in cold calls or outbound messages when the buyer is overloaded with similar outreach and you need to sound more direct and less templated.
steps: Start in a way that sounds honest and slightly different from the usual sales pattern.
Move quickly into relevance so the interrupt has a reason, not just novelty.
Ask a question or offer a point of view that keeps the conversation open.
example: A cold call opens with, 'I know I caught you cold. Give me 30 seconds and then you can decide whether this is relevant,' which feels more honest than a polished company intro.
